results.title: Analysis Results

similarity.title: Similarity Indicators
similarity.tokenOrderedClean: Token Similarity (ordered, excluding comments and whitespace)
similarity.tokenUnorderedFull: Token Similarity (unordered, with comments and whitespace)
similarity.tokenUnorderedClean: Token Similarity (unordered, excluding comments and whitespace)

cloneTypes.title: Clone Detection
cloneTypes.cloneFamiliesActivated: {{detected}} of {{total}} clone families were activated in this run.
cloneTypes.dominantInterpretation: This is the dominant interpretation of the clone evidence raised by the current comparison.
cloneTypes.meaningfulCategoriesSurfaced: The most meaningful detected categories are surfaced first so reviewers can understand the similarity pattern quickly.

exactClone.summary: Nearly identical code with only trivial formatting or whitespace differences.
exactClone.detectedMeaning: This is the strongest duplication signal and often points to direct copy-paste reuse.
exactClone.absentMeaning: The match is not a literal copy; the similarity comes from transformed or adapted logic instead.
exactClone.whyItMatters: Exact duplication is the clearest indicator of copy-paste similarity and usually deserves the highest review attention.

nearMissClone.summary: Mostly the same logic, but with small edits such as changed statements, operators, or conditions.
nearMissClone.detectedMeaning: The code appears to come from the same baseline with light modifications layered on top.
nearMissClone.absentMeaning: The relationship is either more exact than a near-miss or more transformed than simple statement edits.
nearMissClone.whyItMatters: Near-miss matches often hide copied solutions behind minor edits, making them important for manual review.

parameterizedClone.summary: Identifiers, literals, or parameters vary while the structural pattern remains aligned.
parameterizedClone.detectedMeaning: This usually indicates reuse with renamed variables or swapped constant values rather than new logic.
parameterizedClone.absentMeaning: The overlap is not mainly driven by renamed placeholders or parameter substitutions.
parameterizedClone.whyItMatters: This pattern is common when a copied solution is lightly personalized without changing its underlying logic.

functionClone.summary: Functions or routines preserve the same operational pattern or callable behavior.
functionClone.detectedMeaning: The main behavior is replicated at the function level, even if local details changed.
functionClone.absentMeaning: The similarity is not concentrated around function-level reuse as a dominant pattern.
functionClone.whyItMatters: Function-level reuse suggests that entire solution units may have been preserved across the two sources.

nonContiguousClone.summary: Shared logic is split across separated fragments instead of appearing as one continuous block.
nonContiguousClone.detectedMeaning: The duplicated behavior is distributed through the code, which often suggests refactoring or interleaving edits.
nonContiguousClone.absentMeaning: The overlap is more contiguous and appears in larger uninterrupted regions.
nonContiguousClone.whyItMatters: Distributed matching fragments can be harder to spot manually even though they still indicate strong reuse.

structuralClone.summary: Control flow and syntax tree shape align strongly even when exact tokens do not.
structuralClone.detectedMeaning: The two sources are architecturally similar, which is a strong sign of reused solution structure.
structuralClone.absentMeaning: The similarity is driven more by tokens or semantics than by matching syntax-tree structure.
structuralClone.whyItMatters: AST-level alignment is a strong signal when someone preserves the same scaffold but changes surface details.

reorderedClone.summary: Equivalent statements exist, but their order has been rearranged.
reorderedClone.detectedMeaning: The logic was likely preserved while blocks were shuffled to look different or fit a new flow.
reorderedClone.absentMeaning: Statement ordering still differs enough that reordering is not a major explanation for the overlap.
reorderedClone.whyItMatters: Reordering is a common disguise tactic because it changes appearance while preserving most of the logic.

functionReorderedClone.summary: Equivalent functions or callable segments appear in a different arrangement or sequence.
functionReorderedClone.detectedMeaning: The implementation keeps the same functional building blocks while changing their outer organization.
functionReorderedClone.absentMeaning: Function-level rearrangement is not the main pattern explaining the match.
functionReorderedClone.whyItMatters: Function shuffling can make two solutions look original even when the same building blocks are reused.

gappedClone.summary: Shared logic exists with inserted or removed gaps between the matching parts.
gappedClone.detectedMeaning: The clone was modified by adding unrelated code between duplicated fragments without changing the core pattern.
gappedClone.absentMeaning: The overlap is not primarily interrupted by inserted gaps or omissions.
gappedClone.whyItMatters: Inserted gaps often indicate deliberate attempts to break up duplicated logic and reduce obvious matches.

intertwinedClone.summary: Clone fragments are woven together with unrelated statements or alternative logic.
intertwinedClone.detectedMeaning: The original structure appears to have been blended with extra logic, making reuse harder to spot directly.
intertwinedClone.absentMeaning: The clone evidence is cleaner and less entangled with unrelated implementation details.
intertwinedClone.whyItMatters: Intertwined clones are important because they can hide shared logic inside otherwise noisy implementation details.

semanticClone.summary: Behavior is equivalent even when syntax differs noticeably.
semanticClone.detectedMeaning: This is a deeper signal that the two sources may solve the same problem in functionally matching ways.
semanticClone.absentMeaning: The overlap is better explained by syntax and structure than by purely semantic equivalence.
semanticClone.whyItMatters: Semantic matches matter most when two submissions converge on the same behavior despite visible code differences.

fallback.summary: This clone category is part of the detection model used for the current comparison.
fallback.detectedMeaning: The engine found evidence for this clone pattern in the current result.
fallback.absentMeaning: The engine did not find enough evidence for this clone pattern in the current result.
fallback.whyItMatters: This category helps explain how the two sources may relate when viewed through clone-pattern analysis.

cloneProfile.summaryNone: No clone signatures were raised in this run, which means the engine did not find a meaningful duplication pattern across its clone-type checks.
cloneProfile.summaryExact: An exact-clone signal is present, which means the overlap is close to direct duplication rather than only structural resemblance.
cloneProfile.summarySemanticMulti: Multiple transformed clone signatures were detected, including semantic overlap, which suggests reused logic that has been adapted rather than copied verbatim.
cloneProfile.summaryMulti: Several transformed clone patterns were detected, which points to shared implementation roots with renaming, reordering, or local edits applied.
cloneProfile.summarySmall: A smaller set of clone signatures was detected, suggesting selective reuse rather than broad one-to-one duplication.
cloneProfile.noFamiliesActivated: No clone families were activated in this run.

results.noResults: No analysis results available. Run an analysis first.
results.summaryDescription: This result combines text, token, graph, clone-pattern, and AI similarity signals into a single review surface so you can inspect structure and narrative evidence together.
results.overviewDescription: Inspect both source inputs directly inside the overview before moving through similarity signals, clone evidence, and the AI narrative.
results.panelError: This panel could not be displayed. The rest of the results are unaffected.
results.noSavedOrActive: No saved or active analysis is available yet.
results.unableToLoad: Unable to load the analysis result.
results.emptyDescription: Run a fresh comparison or reopen one from history to populate the full results workspace.
results.defaultComparisonDescription: Review both submitted sources directly alongside the analytical findings.

quality.headline: Cleaner quality diagnostics for both sources
quality.description: This view now surfaces the most important findings first, summarizes severity distribution, and keeps the raw diagnostic output available only when you need deep inspection.
quality.totalFindingsDesc: Structured issues across both source reviews.
quality.averageScoreDesc: Only shown when both reports include numeric pylint ratings.
quality.healthierIssueDesc: Chosen by the lighter issue footprint in the current diagnostics.
quality.sourceAFallback: No quality notes were returned for Source A.
quality.sourceBFallback: No quality notes were returned for Source B.
quality.findingsDesc: Structured items extracted from the quality report.
quality.criticalDesc: Errors and fatal findings that can break behavior.
quality.warningsDesc: Warnings, refactors, and style debt that need cleanup.
quality.dominantSignalsDesc: The most recurring rule IDs in this source review.
quality.noStructuredFindingsDesc: This source did not produce any structured linter findings in the current quality pass.
quality.noStructuredReported: No structured linter findings were reported for this source.
quality.findingsSummary: {{critical}} critical, {{warning}} warning, {{style}} style, and {{info}} informational findings were identified.

chat.intro: I am grounded in the current analysis context for {{contextLabel}}. Ask about similarity metrics, graphs, clone types, maintainability, or risks.
chat.unavailable: The assistant is unavailable right now.
chat.title: Ask AI about this analysis
chat.description: Context-aware follow-up discussion grounded in the current result set.
chat.suggestion2: What clone type was detected and why?

analysisReport.title: Inter-Code Analysis Report
analysisReport.description: AI-generated explanation of similarity, structure, maintainability, and risks.
analysisReport.empty: No AI report is available for this analysis.

astGraph.compactDescription: Compact AST tree with labeled nodes for direct structural reading.
astGraph.noGraphDescription: This source did not produce AST graph data for the current analysis.
astGraph.fullScreenDescription: Full-screen AST explorer for easier branch tracking, node inspection, and structural scanning.
astGraph.rootNodeDescription: This node is a structural entry point in the AST and anchors one of the main graph branches.
astGraph.nestedNodeDescription: This node belongs to a deeper AST branch and helps explain how the surrounding code is structurally composed.
astGraph.clickNodeTitle: Click any node to inspect it
astGraph.clickNodeDescription: Selecting a node reveals its identity, line range, depth, and structural connections without leaving the graph view.

diff.title: Line-by-Line Diff
diff.description: Direct side-by-side comparison showing exactly which lines match, differ, or were added.
diff.showingLines: Showing {{count}} lines — scroll to see all differences.

metrics.title: Metrics Comparison
metrics.description: Side-by-side structural and maintainability metrics for both code sources.

pdfExport.title: Export PDF Report
pdfExport.subtitle: Choose which sections to include in the formatted report
pdfExport.previewNote: The report opens in a new tab — use Ctrl+P or the «Save as PDF» button to export.
pdfExport.coverDesc: Title, date, source labels, combined score gauge
pdfExport.executiveSummaryDesc: Key KPIs — score, risk level, clone count, language
pdfExport.similarityMetricsDesc: All similarity dimensions with visual bar charts
pdfExport.cloneDetectionDesc: Full clone-type table with detected/not-detected status
pdfExport.aiStructuredReportDesc: Risk level, verdict, findings, refactoring suggestion
pdfExport.aiAnalysisTextDesc: Full prose AI analysis comparing the two sources
pdfExport.codeQualityDesc: Linter findings, severity breakdown, quality scores
pdfExport.sourceCodeDesc: Full code for both sources (may add pages)

radar.title: Similarity Radar
radar.description: All similarity dimensions at a glance — each axis is an independent measurement.

verdictMeaning.high: Strong similarity. These two sources likely share substantial code — review the flagged clone families before deciding.
verdictMeaning.moderate: Partial similarity. Some shared structure or logic is present; inspect the evidence to judge whether it is meaningful.
verdictMeaning.low: Low similarity. Little shared structure was found; the sources appear largely independent.
verdictMeaning.advisoryNote: This verdict rests mainly on the AI signal, so treat it as advisory rather than definitive.

legend.gateNote: ≥ 80 fails the CI gate by default.
